classmate.name: System
classmate.description: Represents a complete software system as the highest boundary.

business_ontology_properties_list.note: Required; string. Canonical business name used for display and reference. Do not use technical IDs, paths, table names, or implementation details.
Required; string. Defines the semantic boundary and business definition of the node. Do not repeat name and do not include process steps.
Required; string. Capture system-level boundary information: system goals, covered business domains, external system boundaries, global constraints, and governance scope.

Required; string. Defines the semantic boundary and business definition of the node. Do not repeat name and do not include process steps.
Required; string. Capture project-level information: project responsibilities, service boundaries, key upstream/downstream dependencies, and deployment/maintenance ownership boundaries.

classmate.name: Module

business_ontology_properties_list.note: Required; string. Canonical business name used for display and reference. Do not use technical IDs, paths, table names, or implementation details.
Required; string. Defines the semantic boundary and business definition of the node. Do not repeat name and do not include process steps.
Required; string. Capture module-level information: module responsibilities, core capabilities, input/output business objects, and collaboration with other modules.

classmate.name: BusinessObject
classmate.description: Business semantic subject directly processed by the system.

business_ontology_properties_list.note: Required; string. Canonical business name used for display and reference. Do not use technical IDs, paths, table names, or implementation details.
Required; string. Defines the semantic boundary and business definition of the node. Do not repeat name and do not include process steps.
Required; string. Capture business-object semantics: business definition, key identifying elements, lifecycle highlights, and semantic distinctions from other objects.

classmate.name: BusinessProcess
classmate.description: Stable process unit that acts on business objects and drives their creation, change, validation, routing, termination, or recovery within a business context.

business_ontology_properties_list.note: Required; string. Use a concrete business action or process-unit name for display and reference. Prefer names such as 'Create Order', 'Approve Order', 'Allocate Inventory', or 'Confirm Receipt'. Avoid overly broad stage names such as 'Order Management', 'Inbound Process', or other end-to-end flow labels.

Required; string. Defines the semantic boundary and business meaning of the process unit. State which business object it acts on, what business change it performs, and where its responsibility starts and ends. Do not repeat the name and do not expand into a long end-to-end process narrative.

Required; string. Capture process semantics: trigger conditions, target business object, key preconditions, main business rules, produced or consumed artifacts, state changes, and success or failure outcomes.

classmate.name: SourceArtifact
classmate.description: Stable information carrier referenced or produced during processing.

business_ontology_properties_list.note: Required; string. Canonical business name used for display and reference. Do not use technical IDs, paths, table names, or implementation details.
Required; string. Defines the semantic boundary and business definition of the node. Do not repeat name and do not include process steps.
Required; string. Capture artifact semantics: purpose, source and destination, key information carried, and creation/update timing.

classmate.name: APIEndpoint
classmate.description: Stable API contract endpoint that exposes or consumes business capabilities across module, application, or integration boundaries.

business_ontology_properties_list.note: Required; string. Canonical endpoint name used for display and reference. Normalize the OpenAPI summary into a stable business-facing endpoint name instead of using the raw path as the primary display name.
Required; string. Preserve the original OpenAPI summary from the Apifox index so the ontology can serve as a fast API contract index.

Required; string. Integrated business-facing endpoint description distilled from the Apifox operation definition. Explain the business capability and boundary without merely repeating the summary or raw path.

Optional; string. Use only when preserving longer operation context is helpful for fast reuse, such as key request or response semantics, important constraints, or a compact copy of the operation text.
Required; string. Canonical API route template from OpenAPI, such as '/outbound/wms-pick-suggest/rebuild/by-task/{taskId}'.
Required; string. HTTP operation verb from OpenAPI such as GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, or PATCH.

Required; string. Stable local reference to the concrete Apifox operation definition, including file path and method fragment, such as 'apifox/project-4853761-wms-3.0/paths/_wms_outbound_wms-pick-suggest_rebuild_by-task_%7BtaskId%7D.json#post'.

Optional; string. API abstraction layer derived from the Apifox tag and path convention. Use 'level1' for atomic backend APIs that mainly expose raw record fields such as ids, codes, and statuses; use 'level2' for BAM/BFF APIs that expose enriched read models, display names, and aggregated context.

Optional; string. API audience boundary. Use 'internal' for internal WMS APIs used by internal services, apps, or BFF layers; use 'public' for external-facing APIs intended for customers or partners. This property is independent from api_level.

classmate.name: State
classmate.description: Lifecycle phase of an object or process.

business_ontology_properties_list.note: Required; string. Canonical business name used for display and reference. Do not use technical IDs, paths, table names, or implementation details.
Required; string. Defines the semantic boundary and business definition of the node. Do not repeat name and do not include process steps.
Required; string. Capture state semantics: state definition, entry conditions, executable actions, and allowed next states.

classmate.name: Rule
classmate.description: Stable judgment or constraint unit for validation and decisions.

business_ontology_properties_list.note: Required; string. Canonical business name used for display and reference. Do not use technical IDs, paths, table names, or implementation details.
Required; string. Defines the semantic boundary and business definition of the node. Do not repeat name and do not include process steps.
Required; string. Capture rule semantics: rule objective, decision conditions, constrained objects, post-hit actions, and exceptions.

relationships.description: A business object may be structurally composed of another business object.
A rule constrains, validates, or defines the valid conditions of a business object.
A business object maps to source artifacts that carry its information.
An API endpoint may map to controller code, client code, gateway configuration, interface documentation, or integration evidence artifacts.
An API endpoint may depend on rules for request validation, routing decisions, eligibility checks, or rejection behavior.
An API endpoint may trigger, expose, or encapsulate a business process as an interaction boundary.
An API endpoint may query a primary business object, mutate a primary business object, or depend on additional business objects as request context, lookup context, or operational prerequisites.
A business object belongs to or is currently under a state.
A business process may be decomposed into sub-processes, depend on another process as a prerequisite, or flow into the next process as part of an end-to-end business chain.
A business process may act on a business object, depend on a business object as execution context or prerequisite, or produce a downstream business object outcome.
A business process depends on rules for execution or decision logic.
A business process creates, consumes, validates, updates, or is evidenced by source artifacts.
A rule may be evidenced by or directly mapped to source artifacts that encode or document its logic.
A business process drives or participates in state progression.
A module acts on a business object from implementation side.
A module carries or implements a business process.
A module may expose, proxy, implement, or consume an API endpoint.
A project is composed of modules.
A state depends on rules for transition or validation semantics.
A system is composed of projects.

properties.name: ActionDefinition
properties.description: Defines an automated action that can be triggered by events in the system. Types include Python scripts, HTTP API calls, and SendEmail. Reusable across multiple trigger points.
properties.content: ActionDefinition is the automation engine of FlowFlex. Properties: ActionCode, ActionName, ActionType (Python/HttpApi/SendEmail), Description, ActionConfig (JSONB - execution parameters varying by type), IsEnabled, IsTools, IsAIGenerated, TriggerType (Stage/Task/Question/Workflow/Integration). Actions execute when triggered by stage completion, task completion, or questionnaire answers via ActionTriggerMapping. Database table: ff_action_definitions.

properties.name: Action Enabled
properties.description: Action is enabled and can be triggered.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created with IsEnabled=true or re-enabled. Action will execute when matching trigger events occur. Exit condition: IsEnabled set to false (disabled).

properties.name: Action Disabled
properties.description: Action is disabled and will not execute.
properties.content: Entry condition: IsEnabled set to false. Action exists but will not execute even when trigger events occur. Exit condition: IsEnabled set to true (re-enabled).

properties.name: ActionExecution
properties.description: Records the execution of an ActionDefinition triggered by an ActionTriggerMapping. Tracks execution status, timing, input/output, and errors.
properties.content: ActionExecution is the runtime execution record for actions. Properties: ActionDefinitionId, ExecutionId (unique string), ActionTriggerMappingId, ActionName, ActionType, TriggerContext (JSONB - event context that triggered execution), ExecutionStatus (Pending/Running/Completed/Failed/Cancelled/Timeout/Retrying), StartedAt, CompletedAt, DurationMs, ExecutionInput (JSONB), ExecutionOutput (JSONB), ErrorMessage, ErrorStackTrace, ExecutorInfo (JSONB). Database table: ff_action_executions.

properties.name: Execution Pending
properties.description: Action execution created but not yet started.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created with default status Pending. Exit condition: Execution starts (Running).

properties.name: Execution Running
properties.description: Action is currently executing.
properties.content: Entry condition: Execution engine picks up the action. StartedAt is set. Exit condition: Completed, Failed, Cancelled, or Timeout.

properties.name: Execution Completed
properties.description: Action executed successfully.
properties.content: Entry condition: Action finishes without error. CompletedAt and DurationMs set. ExecutionOutput contains result. Terminal state.

properties.name: Execution Failed
properties.description: Action execution failed with error.
properties.content: Entry condition: Action throws error during execution. ErrorMessage and ErrorStackTrace captured. Exit condition: May transition to Retrying.

properties.name: Execution Timeout
properties.description: Action exceeded its timeout threshold.
properties.content: Entry condition: Execution duration exceeds configured timeout. Terminal state.

properties.name: Execution Retrying
properties.description: Action is being retried after failure.
properties.content: Entry condition: Retry logic triggered after Failed state. Exit condition: Completed, Failed, or Timeout on retry.

properties.name: ActionTriggerMapping
properties.description: Maps an ActionDefinition to a specific trigger source (Stage, Task, or Question) with event type, conditions, and execution order. Enables reusable action-to-event binding.
properties.content: ActionTriggerMapping connects ActionDefinitions to trigger sources. Properties: ActionDefinitionId, TriggerType (Stage/Task/Question), TriggerSourceId, WorkFlowId, StageId, TriggerEvent (Completed/Created/Updated/Answered), IsEnabled, ExecutionOrder (smaller first), TriggerConditions (JSONB - conditional execution), MappingConfig (JSONB - custom parameters), Description. Multiple mappings on same trigger execute in ExecutionOrder. Database table: ff_action_trigger_mappings.

properties.name: Checklist
properties.description: A task list template or instance containing ordered ChecklistTasks. Assigned to Stages via Stage Components. Supports template/instance pattern.
properties.content: Checklist is a container for ChecklistTasks. Type can be Template or Instance. Assigned to stages via components_json (a checklist can only be assigned to one stage within the same workflow). Properties: Name, Description, Team, Type (Template/Instance), Status (Active/Inactive), IsTemplate, TemplateId, EstimatedHours, IsActive. Database table: ff_checklist.

properties.name: ChecklistTask
properties.description: An individual task item within a Checklist. Supports task dependencies, assignees, priorities, due dates, and action bindings.
properties.content: ChecklistTask is the execution unit within a Checklist. Properties: Name, Description, TaskType (Manual/Automatic/Document/Approval), IsCompleted, IsRequired, AssigneeId, AssigneeName, AssignedTeam, Priority (Low/Medium/High/Critical), Order, EstimatedHours, ActualHours, DueDate, CompletedDate, CompletionNotes, DependsOnTaskId (prerequisite task), Status (Pending/InProgress/Completed/Blocked/Cancelled), ActionId, ActionMappingId. Supports task dependencies via DependsOnTaskId with circular dependency validation. Database table: ff_checklist_task.

properties.name: Task Pending
properties.description: Task created but not started.
properties.content: Entry condition: Task created or uncompleted (reverted from Completed). Initial state. Exit condition: Task started (InProgress) or directly completed (Completed).

properties.name: Task InProgress
properties.description: Task is being worked on.
properties.content: Entry condition: Task started by assignee. Exit condition: Task completed, blocked, or cancelled.

properties.name: Task Completed
properties.description: Task has been completed with optional notes and actual hours.
properties.content: Entry condition: CompleteTaskAsync with dependency check passed (CanCompleteAsync). Sets IsCompleted=true, CompletedDate, CompletionNotes, ActualHours, Status=Completed. Exit condition: UncompleteTaskAsync (if no completed dependents exist).

properties.name: Task Blocked
properties.description: Task is blocked by dependency or external factor.
properties.content: Entry condition: Dependent task (DependsOnTaskId) not completed. Exit condition: Dependency resolved (dependent task completed).

properties.name: ChecklistTaskCompletion
properties.description: Per-onboarding completion record for a checklist task. Tracks whether a specific task is completed for a specific onboarding instance.
properties.content: ChecklistTaskCompletion bridges ChecklistTask (template) and Onboarding (instance). Allows same task definition to have different completion states per onboarding. Properties: OnboardingId, LeadId, ChecklistId, TaskId, StageId, IsCompleted, CompletedTime, CompletionNotes, FilesJson (file attachments), Source (customer_portal/admin/integration), IpAddress, UserAgent. Database table: ff_checklist_task_completion.

properties.name: DynamicData
properties.description: Dynamic data system consisting of DefineField (field definitions) and BusinessData (field values). Enables configurable custom fields per module without schema changes.
properties.content: DynamicData is a meta-field system. DefineField defines field metadata: ModuleId, FieldName, Description, DataType (text/number/date/select/multi-select etc.), SourceType, IsSystemDefine, IsStatic, IsDisplayField, IsMustUse, IsRequired, IsHidden, IsComputed, AllowEdit, Sort, AdditionalInfo (JSONB). BusinessData stores actual values as JSONB. FieldGroup organizes fields. Supports computed fields, static fields, system-defined fields. Database tables: ff_define_field, ff_business_data, ff_data_value, ff_field_group.

properties.name: EmailBinding
properties.description: Manages email account bindings for Outlook integration. Tracks OAuth tokens, sync state, and delta links for incremental email synchronization.
properties.content: EmailBinding connects a user's email account to FlowFlex for email sync. Properties: UserId, Email, Provider (e.g. Microsoft), AccessToken, RefreshToken, TokenExpireTime, LastSyncTime, SyncStatus, LastSyncError, AutoSyncEnabled, SyncIntervalMinutes, DeltaLinkInbox, DeltaLinkSent, DeltaLinkDeleted. Delta links enable incremental sync via Microsoft Graph API. Database table: ff_email_bindings.

properties.name: EntityMapping
properties.description: Maps external system entity types to FlowFlex entity types for integration data sync. Defines which external entities map to which workflows.
properties.content: EntityMapping links external entity types to FlowFlex. Properties: IntegrationId, SystemId (auto-generated snowflake for external reference), ExternalEntityName, ExternalEntityType, WfeEntityType, WorkflowIds (JSON array), IsActive. Each mapping links an external entity type to WFE entity type and specific workflow IDs. Database table: ff_entity_mapping.

properties.name: Event
properties.description: System event record for tracking business events, supporting event sourcing patterns with retry logic and metadata.
properties.content: Event captures business events in FlowFlex. Properties: EventId (unique string), EventType, EventVersion, EventTimestamp, AggregateId, AggregateType, EventSource, EventData (JSONB), EventMetadata (JSONB), EventDescription, EventStatus, ProcessCount, LastProcessedAt, ErrorMessage, RelatedEntityId, RelatedEntityType, EventTags (JSONB), RequiresRetry, NextRetryAt, MaxRetryCount. Supports retry logic for failed event processing. Database table: ff_events.

properties.name: FieldMapping
properties.description: Maps fields between an external system and FlowFlex for integration data synchronization. Defines field-level mapping with sync direction and type.
properties.content: FieldMapping defines how individual fields map between external systems and FlowFlex during data sync. Properties: ActionId (links to IntegrationAction), ExternalFieldName, WfeFieldId, FieldType (enum), SyncDirection (enum - inbound/outbound/bidirectional), SortOrder, IsRequired, DefaultValue. Essential for Sync Integration Data process to know which external fields correspond to which FlowFlex fields. Database table: ff_inbound_field_mapping.

properties.name: Integration
properties.description: Represents a connection to an external system (CRM, ERP, Marketing, etc.). Manages authentication, entity mappings, field mappings, and data synchronization.
properties.content: Integration connects FlowFlex to external systems. Properties: Type (CRM/ERP/Marketing), Name, Description, Status (Connected/Disconnected/Error/Pending), SystemName, EndpointUrl, AuthMethod (OAuth2/ApiKey/BasicAuth), EncryptedCredentials, LastSyncDate, ErrorMessage, InboundAttachments (JSON), OutboundAttachments (JSON). Has EntityMappings, IntegrationActions, QuickLinks. Database table: ff_integration.

properties.name: IntegrationAction
properties.description: Defines an action bound to an Integration that can be executed against the external system. Supports assignment to specific workflows.
properties.content: IntegrationAction represents executable operations on an external system via Integration. Properties: IntegrationId, ActionCode, ActionName, ActionType (enum), Status (enum), AssignedWorkflowIds (JSON array - which workflows can use this action), Configuration (JSON - action-specific config). Links FieldMappings to define data transformation. Database table: ff_integration_action.

properties.name: InternalNote
properties.description: Internal team notes on an Onboarding or Stage. Supports threading (ParentNoteId), mentions, resolution tracking, and visibility control.
properties.content: InternalNote is for team collaboration. Properties: OnboardingId, StageId (optional), Title, Content, NoteType (General/Important/Warning/Question), Priority (Low/Normal/High/Critical), IsResolved, ResolvedTime, ResolvedById, ResolvedBy, ResolutionNotes, Tags (JSONB), Visibility (Public/Internal/Private), MentionedUserIds (JSONB), Author, AuthorId, ParentNoteId (reply threading), Source. Database table: ff_internal_notes.

properties.name: Message
properties.description: Unified message entity supporting Internal messages, Email (via Outlook integration), and Portal messages. Supports threading, folders, labels, attachments, and read/star/archive states.
properties.content: Message is the unified communication entity. Properties: Subject, Body, BodyPreview, MessageType (Internal/Email/Portal), Folder (Inbox/Sent/Starred/Archive/Trash), Labels (JSONB), SenderId, SenderName, SenderEmail, Recipients (JSONB), CcRecipients, BccRecipients, RelatedEntityType, RelatedEntityId, RelatedEntityCode, IsRead, IsStarred, IsArchived, IsDraft, HasAttachments, SentDate, ReceivedDate, ParentMessageId (threading), ConversationId (grouping), OwnerId (mailbox owner), OriginalFolder (for restore). Database table: ff_messages.

properties.name: MessageAttachment
properties.description: File attachment associated with a Message. Supports both regular attachments and inline content (e.g., embedded images).
properties.content: MessageAttachment stores file metadata for message attachments. Properties: MessageId, FileName, FileSize, ContentType, StoragePath, ExternalAttachmentId (for synced email attachments), ContentId (for inline images referenced in HTML body), IsInline (distinguishes inline vs regular attachment). Database table: ff_message_attachments.

properties.name: Onboarding
properties.description: A customer/lead onboarding workflow instance (also called "Case" in UI and permission system). Each Onboarding is created from a Workflow template and tracks the progress of a specific customer through all stages.
properties.content: Onboarding is the core runtime business entity in FlowFlex. It tracks stage progress (CurrentStageId, CurrentStageOrder, StagesProgressJson), assignees, permissions (team-based and user-based via ViewPermissionSubjectType/OperatePermissionSubjectType toggle), priority, completion rate, ownership, lifecycle dates, and CRM lifecycle stage (LifeCycleStageId/LifeCycleStageName). Linked to CRM leads via LeadId/EntityId. CaseCode is the unique business identifier generated from CaseName. Supports complex permission model with ViewPermissionSubjectType (Team/User), OperatePermissionSubjectType (Team/User), ViewPermissionMode, ViewTeams, ViewUsers, OperateTeams, OperateUsers. Unlike Workflow/Stage which only support Team-based permissions, Onboarding supports both Team-based and User-based permission subjects. Database table: ff_onboarding.

properties.name: Onboarding Inactive
properties.description: Onboarding is created but not yet started. Initial state.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created via CreateAsync with default status "Inactive". Exit condition: StartOnboardingAsync transitions to Active. Only Inactive onboardings can be started. Allows configuration before activation.

properties.name: Onboarding Active
properties.description: Onboarding is actively being processed through stages.
properties.content: Entry condition: StartOnboardingAsync from Inactive, ResumeAsync from Paused, ReactivateAsync from Aborted. Sets StartDate, CurrentStageStartTime. Exit conditions: PauseAsync->Paused, AbortAsync->Aborted, CancelAsync->Cancelled, RejectAsync->Rejected/Terminated, ForceCompleteAsync->Force Completed, natural completion->Completed.

properties.name: Onboarding Paused
properties.description: Onboarding is temporarily paused. Can be resumed.
properties.content: Entry condition: PauseAsync from Active status. Only Active onboardings can be paused. Exit condition: ResumeAsync or ResumeWithConfirmationAsync transitions back to Active.

properties.name: Onboarding Completed
properties.description: All required stages completed naturally. Terminal state.
properties.content: Entry condition: All required stages completed, CompletionRate reaches 100%. Terminal state - no further transitions allowed. ActualCompletionDate is set.

properties.name: Onboarding Force Completed
properties.description: Manually force-completed by admin regardless of stage progress.
properties.content: Entry condition: ForceCompleteAsync with reason. Sets CompletionRate=100, ActualCompletionDate. Terminal state - no further transitions allowed. Requires operate permission.

properties.name: Onboarding Aborted
properties.description: Onboarding terminated prematurely. Can be reactivated.
properties.content: Entry condition: AbortAsync with reason from Active status. Exit condition: ReactivateAsync transitions back to Active. Non-terminal - allows recovery.

properties.name: Onboarding Cancelled
properties.description: Onboarding cancelled. Terminal state, cannot be resumed.
properties.content: Entry condition: CancelAsync with reason. Terminal state - no further transitions allowed. Requires operate permission.

properties.name: Onboarding Rejected
properties.description: Onboarding rejected during review. Terminal state.
properties.content: Entry condition: RejectAsync without TerminateWorkflow flag. Terminal state - no further transitions allowed.

properties.name: Onboarding Terminated
properties.description: Onboarding terminated with workflow termination flag. Terminal state.
properties.content: Entry condition: RejectAsync with TerminateWorkflow=true. Terminal state - no further transitions allowed. Signals that the entire workflow should be considered terminated for this case.

properties.name: OnboardingFile
properties.description: File/attachment associated with a specific Onboarding and optionally a Stage. Supports versioning, categorization, hash-based dedup, and external import tracking.
properties.content: OnboardingFile manages attachments. Properties: OnboardingId, StageId (optional), AttachmentId, OriginalFileName, StoredFileName, FileExtension, FileSize, ContentType, Category (Document/Image/Certificate/Other), Description, IsRequired, Tags, AccessUrl, StoragePath, UploadedById, UploadedByName, UploadedDate, Status (Active/Deleted/Archived), Version, FileHash (dedup), SortOrder, IsExternalImport (excludes from inbound-attachments API), Source (CRM/Portal/Manual). Database table: ff_onboarding_file.

properties.name: Questionnaire
properties.description: A dynamic questionnaire with structured sections and questions (supports multiple types, tables, nesting). Assigned to Stages via components. Supports action trigger mappings on questions/options.
properties.content: Questionnaire is a configurable form template. Structure stored as JSONB (sections with questions). Properties: Name, Description, Status (Draft/Published/Archived), Structure (JSONB), Version, PreviewImageUrl, Category, Tags (JSONB), EstimatedMinutes, TotalQuestions, RequiredQuestions, AllowDraft, AllowMultipleSubmissions, IsActive. Questions can trigger Actions via ActionTriggerMapping. Assigned to stages via components_json. Database table: ff_questionnaire.

properties.name: Questionnaire Draft
properties.description: Questionnaire is being designed. Structure can be modified.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created with default status Draft. Structure can be freely modified. Exit condition: PublishAsync transitions to Published (requires valid structure).

properties.name: Questionnaire Published
properties.description: Questionnaire is active and available for use in onboarding stages.
properties.content: Entry condition: PublishAsync with valid structure (structure is not null). Available for assignment to stages and for filling by customers. Exit condition: ArchiveAsync transitions to Archived.

properties.name: Questionnaire Archived
properties.description: Questionnaire is archived and no longer active.
properties.content: Entry condition: ArchiveAsync. Terminal state - questionnaire is no longer available for new assignments. Existing answers are preserved.

properties.name: QuestionnaireAnswer
properties.description: Stores questionnaire answers for a specific Onboarding+Stage+Questionnaire combination. Supports versioning, draft/submit workflow, and review process.
properties.content: QuestionnaireAnswer stores per-onboarding answers for a questionnaire at a specific stage. Properties: OnboardingId, StageId, QuestionnaireId, Answer (JSONB), Status (Draft/Submitted/Approved), CompletionRate (0-100), CurrentSectionIndex, SubmitTime, ReviewTime, ReviewerId, ReviewNotes, Version, IsLatest, Source (customer_portal/admin/integration), IpAddress, UserAgent. Supports versioning (Version + IsLatest). Database table: ff_questionnaire_answers.

properties.name: Answer Draft
properties.description: Answer being filled, not yet submitted.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created or save draft. Answer can be modified freely. CompletionRate tracks progress per section. Exit condition: Submit action transitions to Submitted.

properties.name: Answer Submitted
properties.description: Answer submitted for review.
properties.content: Entry condition: Submit action. SubmitTime is set. Exit condition: Approved (reviewer approves) or rejected back to Draft.

properties.name: Answer Approved
properties.description: Answer reviewed and approved.
properties.content: Entry condition: Reviewer approves. ReviewTime and ReviewerId set. Terminal state for this version.

properties.name: QuickLink
properties.description: Provides quick navigation links from FlowFlex to external systems. Supports URL parameter placeholders resolved at runtime from onboarding context.
properties.content: QuickLink enables navigation to external systems. Properties: IntegrationId, LinkName, Description, TargetUrl (with parameter placeholders), UrlParameters (JSON array - parameter config), DisplayIcon, RedirectType (new tab/same window), IsActive, SortOrder. URL parameters can source values from onboarding fields (PageParameter, LoginUserInfo). Assigned to stages via components_json. Database table: ff_quick_link.

properties.name: Stage
properties.description: A step/phase within a Workflow. Stages are ordered and contain components (Checklists, Questionnaires, Static Fields, QuickLinks, Files). Each Onboarding instance progresses through stages sequentially.
properties.content: Stage is the structural building block of a Workflow. It defines assignees (DefaultAssignee, CoAssignees), estimated duration, portal visibility/permission, components configuration (components_json), view/operate team permissions, color, and ordering. Stages bridge Workflow templates and Onboarding execution. In the context of an Onboarding, stage progress tracks: Pending -> InProgress -> Completed/Skipped/Rejected/Terminated. Database table: ff_stage.

properties.name: Stage Active
properties.description: Stage is active and participates in workflow execution.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created with IsActive=true or reactivated. Stage is included in onboarding progression and visible in workflow configuration. Exit condition: Deactivated (IsActive set to false) or deleted.

properties.name: Stage Inactive
properties.description: Stage is deactivated and skipped during onboarding progression.
properties.content: Entry condition: IsActive set to false by admin. Stage is excluded from onboarding progression but remains in workflow configuration. Exit condition: IsActive set to true (reactivated).

properties.name: StageCondition
properties.description: Defines condition rules for a stage using Microsoft RulesEngine format. When a stage is completed, conditions are evaluated and configured actions are executed or fallback stage is applied.
properties.content: StageCondition uses Microsoft RulesEngine for condition evaluation on stage completion. Properties: StageId, WorkflowId, Name, Description, RulesJson (JSONB - RulesEngine workflow format), ActionsJson (JSONB - action configurations supporting 7 types: GoToStage, SkipStage, EndWorkflow, SendNotification, UpdateField, TriggerAction, AssignUser), FallbackStageId (redirect when condition not met), IsActive, Status (Valid/Invalid/Draft). One active condition per stage. GoToStage/SkipStage/EndWorkflow are stage control actions and are mutually exclusive (only one can execute per evaluation). Database table: ff_stage_condition.

properties.name: Condition Valid
properties.description: Condition is valid and active, will be evaluated on stage completion.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created with valid rules JSON or rules corrected. IsActive=true, Status=Valid. Will be evaluated when associated stage is completed.

properties.name: Condition Draft
properties.description: Condition is being configured.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created as draft. Status=Draft. Not yet active for evaluation.

properties.name: StaticFieldValue
properties.description: Stores static field values for a specific Onboarding+Stage combination. Supports draft/submit/review workflow with versioning.
properties.content: StaticFieldValue stores per-onboarding field values at a specific stage. Properties: OnboardingId, StageId, FieldName, FieldId, DisplayName, FieldValueJson (JSONB), FieldType, IsRequired, Status (Draft/Submitted), CompletionRate, SubmitTime, ReviewTime, ReviewerId, ReviewNotes, Version, IsLatest, IsSubmitted, Source, ValidationStatus (Pending/Valid/Invalid), ValidationErrors, Metadata (JSONB). Replaces deprecated CustomFieldsJson on Onboarding. Database table: ff_static_field_values.

properties.name: User
properties.description: Portal user entity for FlowFlex. Manages authentication, email verification, team membership, and login tracking.
properties.content: User is the permission subject in FlowFlex. Properties: Username, Email, PasswordHash, EmailVerified, EmailVerificationCode, VerificationCodeExpiry, Status (Pending/Active/Inactive/Locked), LastLoginDate, LastLoginIp, Team. Team field determines team-based permission matching. Linked to IDM (Identity Management) for enterprise SSO. Database table: ff_users.

properties.name: UserInvitation
properties.description: Portal access invitation for external customers. Links a customer email to a specific Onboarding for portal access.
properties.content: UserInvitation manages portal access tokens. Properties: OnboardingId, Email, InvitationToken (unique), Status (Pending/Active/Inactive/Expired/Used), EncryptedAccessToken, SentDate, TokenExpiry, LastAccessDate, ShortUrlId (MD5 hash for compact links), UserId (if user exists), SendCount, InvitationUrl, Notes. One invitation per email per onboarding. Generates encrypted access tokens and short URLs. Database table: ff_user_invitations.

properties.name: Invitation Pending
properties.description: Invitation created but not yet sent or accessed.
properties.content: Entry condition: Created with default status Pending. Exit condition: Sent (email delivered) transitions to Active.

properties.name: Invitation Active
properties.description: Invitation is active and can be used for portal access.
properties.content: Entry condition: Sent and not expired. Exit condition: Deactivated (admin action) or expired (TokenExpiry passed).

properties.name: Invitation Expired
properties.description: Invitation token has expired.
properties.content: Entry condition: Current time > TokenExpiry. Terminal state.

properties.name: Workflow
properties.description: Workflow template that defines the structure of an onboarding process. Acts as a blueprint from which Onboarding instances (Cases) are created. Controls stages, permissions, portal visibility, and lifecycle configuration.
properties.content: Workflow is the top-level configuration entity in FlowFlex. It defines an ordered set of Stages, team-based permission controls (ViewPermissionMode, OperateTeams), portal visibility settings, versioning, and lifecycle dates (StartDate/EndDate). A Workflow can be marked as default (only one at a time). It supports duplication, versioning, activation/deactivation, and automatic expiration. Onboarding instances are created from Workflow templates. Database table: ff_workflow.

properties.name: Workflow Active
properties.description: Workflow is active and available for creating new Onboarding instances.
properties.content: Entry condition: Manual activation via ActivateAsync or creation with default status. IsActive=true, Status="active". Exit condition: Manual deactivation via DeactivateAsync, or automatic expiration when EndDate is reached. Allows creation of new Onboarding instances from this template.

properties.name: Workflow Inactive
properties.description: Workflow is deactivated and cannot be used for new Onboarding instances.
properties.content: Entry condition: Manual deactivation via DeactivateAsync, or automatic expiration processing (ProcessExpiredWorkflowsAsync). IsActive=false, Status="inactive". Exit condition: Manual re-activation via ActivateAsync. Existing Onboarding instances continue to function.

properties.name: Abort Onboarding
properties.description: Terminates onboarding prematurely with reason. Can be reactivated later.
properties.content: Trigger: AbortAsync with reason. Precondition: Status must be "Active". Checks operate permission. Sets Status=Aborted. Appends reason to Notes. Logs operation. Outcome: Status transitions to Aborted.

properties.name: Activate Workflow
properties.description: Sets workflow status to active, making it available for new Onboarding instances.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via ActivateAsync. Sets IsActive=true, Status="active". Preserves permission fields during update. Clears cache, logs operation. Outcome: Workflow transitions from Inactive to Active state.

properties.name: Advance Stage
properties.description: Moves onboarding to the next stage when current stage is completed.
properties.content: Trigger: Stage completion event. Updates CurrentStageId, CurrentStageOrder, CompletionRate. Recalculates completion rate based on (completedStages/totalStages)*100. If all required stages completed, transitions to Completed. Outcome: CurrentStageId and CurrentStageOrder updated, completion rate recalculated.

properties.name: Assign ChecklistTask
properties.description: Assigns task to a specific user with structured assignee info.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via AssignTaskAsync or SetTaskAssigneeAsync. Updates AssigneeId, AssigneeName, AssigneeJson. Outcome: Task assignee updated.

properties.name: Assign Onboarding
properties.description: Assigns onboarding to a specific user and team.
properties.content: Trigger: AssignAsync with input. Updates CurrentAssigneeId, CurrentAssigneeName, CurrentTeam. Outcome: Onboarding assignee info updated.

properties.name: Batch Complete Tasks
properties.description: Completes multiple tasks at once with same completion input.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via BatchCompleteAsync. Validates all tasks exist and can be completed. Completes each task individually. Outcome: Multiple tasks completed.

properties.name: Cancel Onboarding
properties.description: Cancels onboarding with reason. Terminal state.
properties.content: Trigger: CancelAsync with reason. Precondition: Status must not be "Completed" (only completed onboardings cannot be cancelled). Checks operate permission. Sets Status=Cancelled. Appends reason to Notes. Logs operation. Outcome: Status transitions to Cancelled (terminal).

properties.name: Check Resource Permission
properties.description: Unified permission check using STRICT MODE: Final permission = Workflow ∩ Stage ∩ Case (intersection). Admin/Owner bypass all checks.
properties.content: Trigger: Any resource access attempt (view or operate). Steps: 1) Admin bypass (System/Tenant admin). 2) Module-level permission check (IDM). 3) Entity-level permission check (ViewPermissionMode + OperateTeams). For Stage: requires Workflow permission first. For Case: requires Workflow ∩ Stage permission first. Assigned users get special Stage privileges. Portal token bypasses stage permission for PortalAccess endpoints. Outcome: CanView and CanOperate boolean result.

properties.name: Combine Stages
properties.description: Merges multiple stages into one, combining their components.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via CombineStagesAsync. Merges components from source stages into target stage. Removes source stages. Outcome: Single combined stage replaces source stages.

properties.name: Complete ChecklistTask
properties.description: Marks task as completed. Validates dependencies are met first. Updates checklist completion rate.
properties.content: Trigger: User action or automation via CompleteTaskAsync. Precondition: Task not already completed, CanCompleteAsync passes (dependent task must be completed). Sets IsCompleted=true, CompletedDate, Status=Completed. Clears cache. Logs operation. Recalculates checklist completion rate. Outcome: Task status set to Completed.

properties.name: Complete Stage
properties.description: Completes a stage for a specific onboarding. Evaluates stage conditions, executes actions, and may redirect to fallback stage.
properties.content: Trigger: Stage completion event or user action via CompleteStageAsync. Checks stage exists and is active, validates onboarding exists, checks permission. If StageCondition exists: evaluates via RulesEngine. If condition met: executes configured actions. If condition not met and FallbackStageId configured: redirects onboarding to fallback stage. Logs evaluation and action results. Outcome: Stage marked completed, conditions evaluated, next stage determined.

properties.name: Create ChecklistTask
properties.description: Creates a new task within a checklist. Validates dependency cycles, sets order.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via API. Validates no circular dependency (HasCircularDependencyAsync). Auto-sets order if not specified. Creates task in Pending status. Outcome: New task in Pending status.

properties.name: Create New Version
properties.description: Creates a new version of an existing workflow by incrementing the version number and resetting start date.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via CreateNewVersionAsync. Increments Version field, resets StartDate to current date. Clears cache. Outcome: Same workflow entity with incremented version number.

properties.name: Create Onboarding
properties.description: Creates a new onboarding instance from a workflow template. Initializes stage progress, generates case code, sets permissions.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via API. Creates Onboarding in Inactive state from Workflow template. Generates CaseCode from CaseName, initializes StagesProgressJson from workflow stages, sets default permissions. Outcome: New Onboarding in Inactive state with initialized stage progress.

properties.name: Create Onboarding from Integration
properties.description: Creates an onboarding instance from external system data (e.g., CRM lead).
properties.content: Trigger: Integration webhook or sync. Uses EntityMapping to determine target workflow. Creates Onboarding linked to external entity via SystemId/EntityId/IntegrationId. Outcome: New Onboarding linked to external entity.

properties.name: Create Questionnaire
properties.description: Creates a new questionnaire with structure, calculates statistics, and syncs action trigger mappings for questions with actions.
properties.content: Trigger: User action. Creates questionnaire in Draft status. Calculates TotalQuestions and RequiredQuestions from structure. Syncs ActionTriggerMappings for questions that have configured actions. Outcome: New Questionnaire in Draft status.

properties.name: Create Stage
properties.description: Creates a new stage within a workflow. Validates workflow permission, name uniqueness, component uniqueness, and syncs component mappings.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via API. Preconditions: Workflow exists, user has Operate permission on workflow. Validates stage name uniqueness within workflow (ExistsNameInWorkflowAsync), validates component uniqueness (ValidateComponentUniquenessInWorkflowAsync), validates team selections. Creates entity with auto-order if not specified, syncs component mappings within transaction. Logs operation. Outcome: New Stage entity with components and mappings.

properties.name: Create Workflow
properties.description: Creates a new workflow template with stages, permission settings, and optional default designation.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via API. Validates name uniqueness (ExistsNameAsync), validates team selections against IDM team tree, handles default workflow flag (removes previous default if needed), creates entity with stages, syncs component mappings, logs operation. Outcome: New Workflow entity persisted with associated Stages.

properties.name: Deactivate Workflow
properties.description: Sets workflow status to inactive, preventing new Onboarding instances from using it.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via DeactivateAsync. Sets IsActive=false, Status="inactive". Preserves permission fields during update. Clears cache, logs operation. Outcome: Workflow transitions from Active to Inactive state.

properties.name: Delete Stage
properties.description: Soft-deletes a stage. Requires confirmation.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via API with confirm=true. Marks stage as deleted (soft delete). Outcome: Stage marked as deleted.

properties.name: Delete Workflow
properties.description: Deletes a workflow template. Requires confirmation and no existing stages.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via DeleteAsync with confirm=true. Preconditions: No associated stages exist. Clears cache, logs operation. Outcome: Workflow entity removed from database.

properties.name: Duplicate Questionnaire
properties.description: Creates a copy with regenerated IDs in structure JSON.
properties.content: Trigger: User action. Copies questionnaire properties, regenerates all IDs within structure JSON to ensure uniqueness. New copy starts in Draft status. Outcome: New Questionnaire copy in Draft.

properties.name: Duplicate Stage
properties.description: Creates a copy of a stage within the same or different workflow.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via DuplicateAsync. Copies stage properties, generates unique name, preserves components configuration. Outcome: New Stage created as copy.

properties.name: Duplicate Workflow
properties.description: Creates a copy of an existing workflow with all its stages, ensuring unique naming and resetting version.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via DuplicateAsync. Generates unique name, copies workflow properties (resets IsDefault=false, Version=1, UseSameTeamForOperate=true), duplicates all stages preserving order and configuration. Logs operation. Outcome: New Workflow created as copy with duplicated stages.

properties.name: Evaluate Stage Condition
properties.description: When a stage is completed, evaluates the condition using RulesEngine. If met, executes configured actions. If not met, redirects to fallback stage.
properties.content: Trigger: CompleteStageAsync. Finds active StageCondition for the stage. Evaluates RulesJson via RulesEngineService. If condition met: executes ActionsJson via ConditionActionExecutor. If condition not met and FallbackStageId set: updates onboarding CurrentStageId to fallback. Logs evaluation and action results. Outcome: Actions executed OR onboarding redirected.

properties.name: Execute Action
properties.description: Executes an action based on trigger mapping. Runs the configured action (Python/HTTP/Email), records result.
properties.content: Trigger: Event matching ActionTriggerMapping. Creates ActionExecution record. Runs configured action based on ActionType (Python script execution, HTTP API call, or SendEmail). Records Success/Failed status. Outcome: ActionExecution record with result.

properties.name: Force Complete Onboarding
properties.description: Manually completes onboarding regardless of stage progress. Sets completion rate to 100%.
properties.content: Trigger: ForceCompleteAsync with reason. Checks operate permission. Sets Status=Force Completed, CompletionRate=100, ActualCompletionDate=now. Appends reason to Notes. Logs operation. Outcome: Status transitions to Force Completed (terminal).

properties.name: Pause Onboarding
properties.description: Temporarily pauses an active onboarding.
properties.content: Trigger: PauseAsync. Precondition: Status must be "Active". Checks operate permission. Sets Status=Paused. Logs operation. Outcome: Status transitions to Paused.

properties.name: Process Expired Workflows
properties.description: Background job that automatically deactivates workflows whose EndDate has passed.
properties.content: Trigger: Scheduled background task via ProcessExpiredWorkflowsAsync. Finds all active workflows with EndDate < now. Sets Status="inactive", IsActive=false for each. Clears cache per workflow. Outcome: Expired workflows automatically deactivated.

properties.name: Publish Questionnaire
properties.description: Publishes a draft questionnaire making it available for use.
properties.content: Trigger: PublishAsync. Precondition: Structure is not null AND Status != Published. Sets Status=Published. Outcome: Questionnaire available for stage assignment and customer filling.

properties.name: Archive Questionnaire
properties.description: Archives a published questionnaire, making it unavailable for new assignments.
properties.content: Trigger: ArchiveAsync. Precondition: Status is Published. Sets Status=Archived. Existing answers are preserved. Outcome: Questionnaire transitions to Archived (terminal). No longer available for new stage assignments.

properties.name: Submit QuestionnaireAnswer
properties.description: Submits a questionnaire answer for review. Transitions from Draft to Submitted status.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via submit endpoint. Precondition: Answer exists in Draft status with sufficient completion. Sets Status=Submitted, SubmitTime=now. Creates new version if versioning enabled (increments Version, sets IsLatest=true on new, false on previous). May trigger ActionTriggerMappings configured on questions. Outcome: Answer transitions from Draft to Submitted.

properties.name: Reactivate Onboarding
properties.description: Reactivates an aborted onboarding back to active state.
properties.content: Trigger: ReactivateAsync with reason. Precondition: Status must be "Aborted". Checks operate permission. Sets Status=Active. Appends reason to Notes. Logs operation. Outcome: Status transitions to Active.

properties.name: Reject Onboarding
properties.description: Rejects onboarding during review. Can optionally terminate the entire workflow.
properties.content: Trigger: RejectAsync with input. Precondition: Status must not be "Completed", "Rejected", or "Terminated". Checks operate permission. If TerminateWorkflow=true: Status=Terminated. Otherwise: Status=Rejected. Both are terminal states. Logs operation. Outcome: Status transitions to Rejected or Terminated.

properties.name: Resume Onboarding
properties.description: Resumes a paused onboarding back to active state.
properties.content: Trigger: ResumeAsync or ResumeWithConfirmationAsync. Precondition: Status must be "Paused". Checks operate permission. Sets Status=Active. ResumeWithConfirmation allows reason and notes. Logs operation. Outcome: Status transitions to Active.

properties.name: Sort ChecklistTasks
properties.description: Reorders tasks within a checklist.
properties.content: Trigger: User drag-and-drop via SortTasksAsync. Updates order_index values for specified tasks. Outcome: Task order updated.

properties.name: Sort Stages
properties.description: Reorders stages within a workflow by updating order_index values.
properties.content: Trigger: User drag-and-drop or API via SortStagesAsync. Updates order_index for all affected stages based on new ordering. Outcome: Stage order updated for all affected stages within the workflow.

properties.name: Start Onboarding
properties.description: Activates an inactive onboarding, setting start date and optionally resetting progress.
properties.content: Trigger: StartOnboardingAsync. Precondition: Status must be "Inactive". Checks operate permission (EnsureCaseOperatePermissionAsync). Sets Status=Active, StartDate=now, CurrentStageStartTime=now. Optionally resets progress if input.ResetProgress=true. Appends to Notes. Logs operation. Outcome: Status transitions to Active.

properties.name: Sync Integration Data
properties.description: Synchronizes data between FlowFlex and external system based on entity/field mappings.
properties.content: Trigger: Manual or scheduled sync. Uses Integration authentication config to connect. Reads EntityMapping to determine which external entities map to which workflows. Syncs data bidirectionally based on field mappings. Updates LastSyncDate. Outcome: Data synchronized between systems.

properties.name: Trigger Action Event
properties.description: When a stage/task/question event occurs, finds matching ActionTriggerMappings and queues action executions in order.
properties.content: Trigger: Stage completed, Task completed, Question answered. Finds all enabled ActionTriggerMappings matching the trigger source and event. Evaluates trigger_conditions. Queues matching actions for execution in execution_order. Outcome: Matching actions queued for execution.

properties.name: Uncomplete ChecklistTask
properties.description: Reverts a completed task to Pending. Blocked if dependent tasks are already completed.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via UncompleteTaskAsync. Precondition: Task is completed AND no completed dependent tasks exist (GetDependentTasksAsync check). Sets IsCompleted=false, CompletedDate=null, Status=Pending. Recalculates checklist completion rate. Outcome: Task reverted to Pending.

properties.name: Update Stage
properties.description: Updates stage configuration including name, assignees, components, permissions, and portal settings.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via API. Validates name uniqueness if changed, validates component uniqueness, validates team selections. Detects changes, updates entity, syncs component mappings if components changed, syncs assignees to existing onboardings if assignee changed. Clears cache, logs operation with change details. Outcome: Stage entity updated, mappings synced.

properties.name: Update Stage Components
properties.description: Updates the components configuration (checklists, questionnaires, static fields, files, quicklinks) for a stage.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via UpdateComponentsAsync. Validates component uniqueness within workflow. Updates components_json, syncs component mapping tables. Outcome: Components JSON updated, mappings synced.

properties.name: Update Workflow
properties.description: Updates workflow configuration including name, description, stages, permissions, and portal settings.
properties.content: Trigger: User action via UpdateAsync. Validates name uniqueness (if changed), validates team selections, detects changes, updates entity, syncs component mappings if stages changed, clears cache, logs operation with change details. Outcome: Workflow entity updated.

Defines: ActionDefinitionId, ExecutionId, ActionTriggerMappingId, ActionName, ActionType, TriggerContext (JSONB), ExecutionStatus, StartedAt, CompletedAt, DurationMs, ExecutionInput (JSONB), ExecutionOutput (JSONB), ErrorMessage, ErrorStackTrace, ExecutorInfo (JSONB). Maps to ff_action_executions.

properties.name: Checklist.cs

Maps to ff_checklist table. Assignments are now managed through Stage Components only (legacy AssignmentDto deprecated).

properties.name: ChecklistTask.cs
properties.description: Domain entity for ChecklistTask with all properties.
properties.content: Path: packages/flowFlex-backend/Domain/Entities/OW/ChecklistTask.cs. Defines ChecklistTask: ChecklistId, Name, Description, TaskType, IsCompleted, IsRequired, AssigneeId, AssigneeName, AssignedTeam, Priority, Order, EstimatedHours, ActualHours, DueDate, CompletedDate, CompletionNotes, DependsOnTaskId, AttachmentsJson, AssigneeJson, Status, IsActive, ActionId, ActionName, ActionMappingId.

properties.name: ChecklistTaskCompletion.cs
properties.description: Domain entity for per-onboarding task completion records.
properties.content: Path: packages/flowFlex-backend/Domain/Entities/OW/ChecklistTaskCompletion.cs. Defines: OnboardingId, LeadId, ChecklistId, TaskId, StageId, IsCompleted, CompletedTime, CompletionNotes, FilesJson, Source, IpAddress, UserAgent. Maps to ff_checklist_task_completion.

properties.name: ChecklistTaskService.cs
properties.description: Service with task CRUD, completion, dependency logic, batch operations.
properties.content: Path: packages/flowFlex-backend/Application/Services/OW/ChecklistTaskService.cs. Implements CreateAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync, CompleteTaskAsync, UncompleteTaskAsync, BatchCompleteAsync, SortTasksAsync, AssignTaskAsync, SetTaskAssigneeAsync. Validates dependencies (HasCircularDependencyAsync, CanCompleteAsync), manages completion rate recalculation.

properties.name: DefineField.cs
properties.description: Domain entity for field definitions in the dynamic data system.
properties.content: Path: packages/flowFlex-backend/Domain/Entities/DynamicData/DefineField.cs. Defines: ModuleId, FieldName, Description, DataType, SourceType, SourceName, FormatId, ValidateId, RefFieldId, IsSystemDefine, IsStatic, IsDisplayField, IsMustUse, IsRequired, IsTableMustShow, IsHidden, IsComputed, AllowEdit, AllowEditItem, Sort, AdditionalInfo (JSONB). Maps to ff_define_field.

properties.name: BusinessData.cs
properties.description: Domain entity for dynamic business data records.
properties.content: Path: packages/flowFlex-backend/Domain/Entities/DynamicData/BusinessData.cs. Defines: ModuleId, InternalData (JSONB). Stores per-module business data records with flexible JSON structure. Maps to ff_business_data.

properties.name: DataValue.cs
properties.description: Domain entity for individual field values in the dynamic data system.

Defines: ModuleId, BusinessId, FieldId, FieldName, DataType, LongValue, IntValue, DoubleValue, TextValue, Varchar100Value, Varchar500Value, VarcharValue, BoolValue, DateTimeValue, StringListValue (JSONB). Uses typed columns for efficient querying by data type. Maps to ff_data_value.

properties.name: FieldGroup.cs

properties.content: Path: packages/flowFlex-backend/Domain/Entities/DynamicData/FieldGroup.cs. Defines: ModuleId, GroupName, Sort, IsSystemDefine, IsDefault, Fields (bigint array - field IDs in this group). Maps to ff_field_group.

properties.name: EntityMapping.cs

properties.content: Path: packages/flowFlex-backend/Domain/Entities/Integration/EntityMapping.cs. Defines: IntegrationId, SystemId, ExternalEntityName, ExternalEntityType, WfeEntityType, WorkflowIds (JSON array), IsActive. Maps to ff_entity_mapping.

properties.name: FieldMapping.cs

properties.name: IntegrationAction.cs

properties.name: Integration.cs

Defines: Type, Name, Description, Status (IntegrationStatus enum), SystemName, EndpointUrl, AuthMethod (AuthenticationMethod enum), EncryptedCredentials, LastSyncDate, ErrorMessage, InboundAttachments, OutboundAttachments. Maps to ff_integration.

properties.name: InternalNote.cs

properties.name: Message.cs

properties.name: OnboardingCrudService.cs

properties.name: OnboardingStatusService.cs

Implements StartOnboardingAsync, PauseAsync, ResumeAsync, ResumeWithConfirmationAsync, CancelAsync, AbortAsync, ReactivateAsync, RejectAsync, ForceCompleteAsync, AssignAsync, UpdateCompletionRateAsync. Each transition validates current status, checks operate permission, updates entity, logs operation.

properties.name: PermissionService.cs

properties.content: Path: packages/flowFlex-backend/Application/Services/OW/PermissionService.cs. Coordinates three-level permission check. Delegates to WorkflowPermissionService, StagePermissionService, CasePermissionService. Admin/Owner bypass. Portal token bypass for PortalAccess endpoints.

properties.name: QuestionnaireAnswer.cs

properties.name: Action Execution Order
properties.description: When multiple mappings match the same trigger, they execute in order of execution_order field (smaller first).
properties.content: Condition: Multiple mappings on same trigger source. Effect: Actions execute sequentially by execution_order (ascending). Evidence: ActionTriggerMapping.ExecutionOrder property.

properties.name: Action Failure Non-Blocking
properties.description: A single action execution failure does not prevent subsequent actions in the same trigger from executing.
properties.content: Condition: An action in a sequence fails (throws error or times out). Effect: Error is logged and recorded in ActionExecution, but remaining actions in the execution_order sequence continue to execute. This is a deliberate fault-tolerance design — one broken action should not block the entire workflow progression. Evidence: ConditionActionExecutor continues executing remaining actions even if one fails.

properties.name: Action Trigger Condition
properties.description: ActionTriggerMapping can have trigger_conditions JSON that must evaluate to true before the action executes.
properties.content: Condition: trigger_conditions evaluates to true or is empty/null. Effect: Action execution proceeds if condition met, skipped if not. Evidence: ActionTriggerMapping.TriggerConditions JSONB field.

properties.name: Action Type Configuration
properties.description: ActionDefinition.ActionType determines execution method and config schema.
properties.content: ActionType determines execution engine: Python (executes Python script), HttpApi (calls external HTTP endpoint), SendEmail (sends email via template). Each type has different action_config JSON schema. Python needs script/params, HttpApi needs url/method/headers/body, SendEmail needs template/recipients/subject. Validated at execution time.

properties.name: Admin Permission Bypass
properties.description: System Admin and Tenant Admin bypass all permission checks entirely.
properties.content: Condition: User role is System or Tenant admin (checked via PermissionHelpers.IsSystemAdmin/IsTenantAdmin). Effect: All permission checks return CanView=true, CanOperate=true immediately. Evidence: PermissionService checks admin bypass as Step 0 before any other logic.

properties.name: Case Code Generation
properties.description: Unique case code generated from CaseName on creation. Used as primary business identifier.
properties.content: Condition: Onboarding creation or legacy data migration. CaseCode is auto-generated from CaseName via ICaseCodeGeneratorService.GenerateCaseCodeAsync. Used as primary business identifier in UI and APIs. Evidence: OnboardingUserManagementService.EnsureCaseCodeAsync.

properties.name: Component Portal Access Control
properties.description: Each stage component has CustomerPortalAccess setting (Viewable/Completable/NotAvailable) controlling what external customers can see and do in the portal.
properties.content: CustomerPortalAccess enum per component: Viewable (customer can see but not modify), Completable (customer can fill/complete), NotAvailable (hidden from portal). Applied independently per component within a stage. Evidence: StageComponent.CustomerPortalAccess property.

properties.name: Component Uniqueness Within Workflow
properties.description: A checklist or questionnaire can only be assigned to one stage within the same workflow.
properties.content: Condition: ValidateComponentUniquenessInWorkflowAsync passes. Checks that no checklist ID or questionnaire ID appears in another stage of the same workflow. Effect: Throws error if component already assigned to another stage in same workflow. Evidence: StageService.ValidateComponentUniquenessInWorkflowAsync.

properties.name: Condition Fallback Stage
properties.description: When condition is not met and FallbackStageId is configured, onboarding is redirected to the fallback stage.
properties.content: Condition: Condition evaluation returns false AND FallbackStageId is not null. Effect: Onboarding CurrentStageId is updated to FallbackStageId instead of advancing normally. Evidence: StageService.CompleteStageAsync redirects to fallback stage when condition not met.

properties.name: ConditionAction Types
properties.description: StageCondition ActionsJson supports 7 action types: GoToStage, SkipStage, EndWorkflow, SendNotification, UpdateField, TriggerAction, AssignUser.
properties.content: Each action type has specific behavior: GoToStage (jump to specific stage), SkipStage (skip next N stages), EndWorkflow (force complete the onboarding), SendNotification (send email to users/teams), UpdateField (update onboarding field value), TriggerAction (execute a predefined ActionDefinition via HTTP), AssignUser (assign user/team to a stage). Actions execute sequentially by order field. Per-type timeouts: default 30s, SendNotification 60s, TriggerAction 45s. TriggerAction results are passed as prev_ prefixed fields to subsequent actions (chaining). Evidence: ConditionActionExecutor.cs, StageConditionConstants.cs.

properties.name: ConditionAction Stage Control Mutual Exclusion
properties.description: GoToStage, SkipStage, and EndWorkflow are stage control actions — only one can execute per condition evaluation.
properties.content: Condition: ActionsJson contains multiple stage control actions (GoToStage/SkipStage/EndWorkflow). Effect: Only one stage control action is allowed per condition. These three actions directly affect stage progression and are mutually exclusive — GoToStage redirects, SkipStage advances past stages, EndWorkflow terminates. Other action types (SendNotification, UpdateField, TriggerAction, AssignUser) can execute alongside any stage control action. Evidence: StageConditionConstants.cs categorizes these as StageControlActions.

properties.name: Integration Authentication Methods
properties.description: Integration.AuthMethod determines auth strategy: OAuth2 (token refresh flow), ApiKey (header-based), BasicAuth (username/password).
properties.content: AuthMethod enum determines how API calls to external system are authenticated. Credentials stored encrypted in EncryptedCredentials field. OAuth2: requires token endpoint, client_id, client_secret, handles token refresh. ApiKey: sends key in configured header. BasicAuth: sends username/password in Authorization header. Evidence: Integration.cs AuthMethod property and IntegrationService authentication logic.

properties.name: No Circular Dependencies
properties.description: Task dependency chain must not form a cycle. Validated recursively on create/update.
properties.content: Condition: HasCircularDependencyAsync returns false. Recursively checks dependency chain to detect cycles. Effect: Throws error if circular dependency detected. Evidence: ChecklistTaskService.HasCircularDependencyAsync and HasCircularDependencyRecursiveAsync.

properties.name: Onboarding Completion Rate Calculation
properties.description: Completion rate is calculated as (completedStages / totalStages) * 100.
properties.content: Condition: Stage advancement or force completion. CompletionRate = (CurrentStageOrder / TotalStages) * 100. ForceComplete sets CompletionRate=100 directly. Evidence: OnboardingStatusService.UpdateCompletionRateAsync and ForceCompleteAsync.

properties.name: Onboarding Operate Permission
properties.description: All status operations require operate permission check via EnsureCaseOperatePermissionAsync before execution.
properties.content: Condition: User has operate permission on the case (checked via EnsureCaseOperatePermissionAsync). Effect: Throws permission error if not authorized. Applied to: Start, Pause, Resume, Abort, Reactivate, Cancel, Reject, ForceComplete. Evidence: Every status method in OnboardingStatusService calls EnsureCaseOperatePermissionAsync first.

properties.name: Onboarding Status Transition Guard
properties.description: Each status operation validates current status before allowing transition.
properties.content: Start: only from Inactive. Pause: only from Active. Resume: only from Paused. Abort: only from Active. Reactivate: only from Aborted. Cancel: only blocked from Completed. Reject: blocked from Completed/Rejected/Terminated. ForceComplete: blocked from Completed/Force Completed. Effect: Throws BusinessError if invalid transition attempted. Evidence: OnboardingStatusService validates Status field before each transition.

properties.name: Operate Permission Always Whitelist
properties.description: Operate permission is ALWAYS whitelist-based regardless of ViewPermissionMode. In Public mode, empty OperateTeams means everyone can operate.
properties.content: ViewPermissionMode (blacklist/whitelist) only affects View permissions. Operate is always whitelist: user must be in OperateTeams. Exception: Public mode with empty/null OperateTeams means all users can operate. Evidence: WorkflowPermissionService.CheckOperatePermission comment and logic.

properties.name: Owner Permission Bypass
properties.description: Resource owner (Ownership field on Onboarding, CreateUserId on Workflow) bypasses permission checks for owned resources.
properties.content: Condition: User ID matches resource owner. For Workflow: CreateUserId. For Onboarding: Ownership field. Effect: Permission checks return true for owned resource. Evidence: PermissionHelpers.IsCurrentUserOwner and Private mode logic.

properties.name: Permission Strict Mode (Intersection)
properties.description: Three-level permission intersection: Workflow ∩ Stage ∩ Case. No Workflow permission means no access to Stage or Case.
properties.content: Final permission = Workflow ∩ Stage ∩ Case (intersection). Admin/Owner bypass all checks. Assigned users get special Stage privileges. ViewPermissionMode controls visibility: Public, VisibleToTeams, InvisibleToTeams, Private. At Workflow/Stage level, permissions are always Team-based (ViewTeams/OperateTeams). At Onboarding (Case) level, ViewPermissionSubjectType and OperatePermissionSubjectType (Team/User enum) determine whether the system checks Teams or Users fields. When SubjectType=Team, ViewTeams/OperateTeams are used; when SubjectType=User, ViewUsers/OperateUsers are used. Evidence: PermissionService.cs implements strict intersection mode, CasePermissionService checks SubjectType.

properties.name: Portal Token Bypass
properties.description: Portal users accessing PortalAccess endpoints bypass stage permission checks.
properties.content: Condition: Request has portal token AND endpoint is marked with [PortalAccess] attribute. Effect: Stage permission check returns success immediately. Evidence: PermissionService.CheckStageAccessAsync checks IsPortalTokenWithPortalAccess.

properties.name: Questionnaire Publish Guard
properties.description: Cannot publish without valid structure JSON. Cannot publish if already published.
properties.content: Condition: Structure is not null AND Status != Published. Effect: Blocks publish if no structure exists or already published. Evidence: QuestionnaireService.PublishAsync validates structure before status change.

properties.name: Single Default Workflow
properties.description: Only one workflow can be marked as default at any time.
properties.content: Condition: IsDefault=true on create/update. Effect: Previous default workflow has IsDefault set to false via RemoveDefaultWorkflowAsync before new default is set. Evidence: WorkflowService.CreateAsync checks and removes existing default.

properties.name: Stage Component Types
properties.description: Stage components_json supports 5 component keys: fields, checklist, questionnaires, files, quickLinks.
properties.content: Each component has Key, IsEnabled, Order, CustomerPortalAccess (Viewable/Completable/NotAvailable), and type-specific IDs (ChecklistIds, QuestionnaireIds, StaticFieldIds, QuickLinkIds). Components determine what content types are available in a stage. Evidence: StageComponent.cs model and StageService component parsing.

properties.name: Stage Condition Evaluation
properties.description: When a stage is completed, if a StageCondition exists, it is evaluated via RulesEngine.
properties.content: Condition: StageCondition exists and is active for the stage. If condition met: configured actions execute. If condition not met and FallbackStageId configured: onboarding CurrentStageId is updated to fallback stage. Evidence: StageService.CompleteStageAsync evaluates condition via RulesEngineService.

properties.name: Stage Name Uniqueness Within Workflow
properties.description: Stage name must be unique within the same workflow.
properties.content: Condition: ExistsNameInWorkflowAsync returns false for the given name within the workflow. Validated on create and update (when name changes). Effect: Throws BusinessError if duplicate name in same workflow. Evidence: StageService.CreateAsync and UpdateAsync.

properties.name: Stage Workflow Permission
properties.description: User must have Operate permission on the parent workflow to create/modify stages.
properties.content: Condition: CheckWorkflowAccessAsync returns CanOperate=true for the user on the parent workflow. Effect: Throws permission error if not authorized. Evidence: StageService.CreateAsync checks workflow permission before stage creation.

properties.name: Task Dependency Validation
properties.description: A task cannot be completed if its dependent task (DependsOnTaskId) is not yet completed.
properties.content: Condition: DependsOnTaskId is null OR dependent task IsCompleted=true. Checked via CanCompleteAsync. Effect: Blocks completion if dependency not met. Evidence: ChecklistTaskService.CompleteTaskAsync calls CanCompleteAsync.

properties.name: Task Uncomplete Guard
properties.description: A completed task cannot be uncompleted if other tasks depend on it and are already completed.
properties.content: Condition: No completed dependent tasks exist (GetDependentTasksAsync check). Effect: Blocks uncomplete if dependents are completed. Evidence: ChecklistTaskService.UncompleteTaskAsync checks dependent tasks.

properties.name: View Permission Mode Configuration
properties.description: ViewPermissionMode controls visibility: Public (all can view), VisibleToTeams (whitelist), InvisibleToTeams (blacklist), Private (owner only).
properties.content: Applied at Workflow, Stage, and Onboarding levels independently. Public: returns true for all users. VisibleToTeams: user must be in at least one team listed in ViewTeams. InvisibleToTeams: user must NOT be in any team listed in ViewTeams. Private: only the resource creator (CreateUserId) can view. At Workflow/Stage level, ViewTeams is always the checked field (Team-based only). At Onboarding level, ViewPermissionSubjectType determines whether ViewTeams or ViewUsers is checked. Evidence: WorkflowPermissionService.CheckViewPermission switch expression, CasePermissionService.CheckViewPermission checks SubjectType.

properties.name: Workflow Auto-Expiration
properties.description: Workflows with EndDate in the past are automatically set to inactive by background processing.
properties.content: Condition: EndDate < current time AND Status=active AND IsActive=true. Effect: Status transitions to inactive, IsActive set to false. Trigger: ProcessExpiredWorkflowsAsync scheduled task. Evidence: WorkflowService.ProcessExpiredWorkflowsAsync.

properties.name: Workflow Delete Guard
properties.description: Workflows with existing stages cannot be deleted. Deletion requires explicit confirmation.
properties.content: Condition: confirm=true AND no associated stages exist (stages.Any() must be false). Effect: Throws BusinessError if stages exist or confirmation not provided. Evidence: WorkflowService.DeleteAsync checks stages before deletion.

properties.name: Workflow Name Uniqueness
properties.description: Workflow name must be unique within the tenant.
properties.content: Condition: ExistsNameAsync returns false for the given name. Validated on create and update (when name changes). Effect: Throws BusinessError if name already exists. Evidence: WorkflowService.CreateAsync and UpdateAsync both call ExistsNameAsync.

properties.name: Workflow Portal Visibility
properties.description: Workflow.VisibleInPortal controls whether the entire workflow appears in customer portal.
properties.content: Condition: VisibleInPortal=true AND PortalPermission set (Viewable or Completable). Effect: Workflow and its stages appear in customer portal with configured access level. PortalPermission controls interaction level. Evidence: Workflow.cs VisibleInPortal and PortalPermission properties.

properties.name: Workflow Team Permission Control
properties.description: View and operate permissions are controlled by team-based subject types with modes: Public, VisibleToTeams, InvisibleToTeams.
properties.content: ViewPermissionMode determines visibility: Public (all can view), VisibleToTeams (whitelist), InvisibleToTeams (blacklist). OperateTeams defines which teams can perform CRUD operations. Team selections are validated against IDM team tree via ValidateTeamSelectionsAsync. UseSameTeamForOperate synchronizes operate teams with view teams. Evidence: Workflow.cs properties and WorkflowService.ValidateTeamSelectionsAsync.
